back_translation: At that time, the disciples came into the presence of Jesus and asked him, "Who then will be the greatest in the kingdom of heaven?"
Jesus called a little child to come, then set that child in the midst of them.
And he said, "Truly I tell you, if you do not change your mind and become like little children, you will absolutely not be able to enter the kingdom of heaven.
Therefore, whoever humbles himself like this little child — that one is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
And whoever welcomes a little child like this, even just one, in my name, also welcomes me.

But whoever causes one of these little ones who believe in me to stumble and go astray — for that person it would be better to have a large millstone tied around his neck and be sunk down into the deep sea.
Woe to the world because of the things that cause people to go astray! For such things must unavoidably come, but woe to the person through whom the thing that causes stumbling comes!

If your hand or your foot causes you to stumble and go astray, cut it off and throw it away from you. For it is better for you to enter into life crippled or limping lame than to have two hands and two feet complete and be thrown into the eternal fire.

And if your eye causes you to stumble and go astray, pluck it out and throw it away from you. For it is better for you to enter into life with just one eye than to have two eyes complete and be thrown into the fire of hell.
See to it carefully — do not despise even one of these little ones, for I tell you that their angels in heaven continually watch and see the face of my Father who dwells in heaven.
What do you think? If a certain man has a hundred sheep and one of them goes astray, will he not leave the ninety-nine on the mountains and go out to search for the one that has gone astray?
And if he finds that sheep, truly I tell you, he will rejoice over that one more than over the ninety-nine that did not go astray.
In the same way, your Father who dwells in heaven has no will to let even one of these little ones perish.
And if your brother sins against you, go and speak to correct him privately, just the two of you alone between you and him. If he listens to you, then you have gained your brother back.
But if he will not listen, take one or two more with you, so that ‹every word may be confirmed by the mouth of two or three witnesses.›
And if he refuses to listen to those people, report the matter to the church. And if he refuses to listen even to the church, then consider him as a Gentile and a tax collector.
Truly I tell you, whatever you bind on the earth will be bound in heaven also, and whatever you loose on the earth will be loosed in heaven also.
Again, truly I tell you that if two of you agree together wholeheartedly on the earth about anything that they will request, my Father who dwells in heaven will do it for the two of them.
For wherever there are two or three who gather together in my name, there I am in the midst of them."
Then Peter came into his presence and humbly said, "Lord, how many times should I forgive my brother who sins against me? Up to seven times?"
Jesus said to Peter, "I do not tell you up to seven times, but up to seventy-seven times.
For this reason, the kingdom of heaven is like a certain king who desired to settle accounts with his slaves.
When he began to settle accounts, one person who owed ten thousand talents was brought before him.

Because that slave had no means to repay the debt, the king commanded that he himself be sold — together with his wife, his children, and all the property he had — in order to bring the money to settle the debt.
That slave then prostrated himself low at the royal feet of the king and humbly said, ‹Please graciously have patience with me for a little while, and I will pay back the whole debt to you.›
The king of that slave had royal compassion on him, and so he released him and forgave the debt for him.

But when that slave went out, he found one of his fellow slaves who owed him a hundred denarii. So he seized that fellow slave, began to choke him, and said, ‹Pay back the debt that you owe me right now!›
That fellow slave then knelt down and pleaded with him, saying, ‹Please have patience with me for a little while, and I will pay back the debt to you.›
But that slave was not willing; instead, he went out, seized him, and threw him into prison until he could pay back the debt that he owed.
When his fellow slaves saw what had happened, they were exceedingly grieved, and they went into the presence of their king and recounted everything that had happened for him to know.
Then the king summoned that slave and said, ‹You wicked slave! I forgave all that debt for you because you pleaded with me.
Should you not also have had a merciful heart toward your fellow slave, just as I had mercy on you?›
The king was royally angry, and so he handed him over into the hands of the guards who torture prisoners, until he could pay back the whole debt that he owed.
My Father who dwells in heaven will do to you in the very same way, if each one of you does not forgive your brother from your true heart."
